Create a bad function to find the smallest element in an array, and example code. Working but with lots of bad practices

---

_**Agent (copilot/gpt-4.1)**_

Here is a "bad" function to find the smallest element in an array, along with example usage. This function intentionally uses poor practices such as unnecessary variables, poor naming, lack of error handling, and inefficient logic:

```javascript
function baddestSmallest(arr) {
    var i = 0;
    var min = 999999999;
    while (i < arr.length) {
        if (arr[i] < min) {
            min = arr[i];
        }
        i = i + 1;
    }
    return min;
}

// Example usage:
var myArr = [5, 3, 8, 1, 4];
console.log("Smallest is: " + baddestSmallest(myArr));
```

This code works, but it uses magic numbers, poor variable names, and lacks input validation.
